summary: Matched and linked the Kyasarin egg item implementation by changing itkyasarinegg.c from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py, adding its item state table, spawn/setup logic, collision/physics/animation callbacks, damage interaction handlers, and required item-variable and attribute structs. The PR also updated headers and the Item item-var union so kyasarinEgg per-item state could be accessed by name.
what_changed: Marked src/melee/it/items/itkyasarinegg.c as Matching instead of NonMatching in configure.py.

Added ItemStateTable it_803F9328 for the Kyasarin egg, with entries wiring motion callbacks such as itKyasarinegg_UnkMotion0_Coll, itKyasarinegg_UnkMotion1_Phys/Coll, itKyasarinegg_UnkMotion3_Phys/Coll, and itKyasarinegg_UnkMotion4_Anim.

Implemented egg creation/setup routine it_802EFA44, which spawns item kind 0xEC, sets facing_dir and mpColl facing direction, clears/set item flags and kyasarinEgg item vars, and transitions into initial motion setup via it_802EFB0C.
Implemented launch/setup routine it_802EFB0C using special attributes to set initial horizontal velocity and transition to item state 0.

Filled in motion and callback behavior for picked up, dropped, thrown, ground/air physics, collision bounce, shield bounce, damage/clank/shield/reflection handlers, and terminal cleanup/animation state.
Added itKyasarinEgg_ItemVars with padding through ip+DD4, x20 at ip+DF4, and x24 at ip+DF8, and added itKyasarinEggAttributes with fields x0, x4, x8, xC, and x10.
Added kyasarinEgg to the Item xDD4_itemVar union in src/melee/it/types.h.
Adjusted header prototypes to match actual return types: collision callbacks use int/bool as appropriate and motion 4 animation returns bool.
Added includes needed by the new implementation: efsync.h, mpcoll.h, mplib.h, and baselib/random.h.
smart_moves: Promoted the file to Matching only after wiring both implementation and build configuration, ensuring the object is actually linked as matched.

Introduced named item-variable and special-attribute structs instead of relying on raw offsets, making accesses like xDD4_itemVar.kyasarinEgg.x20 and xC4_article_data->x4_specialAttributes reusable and easier to audit.

Shared identical damage-response behavior across several callbacks through an inline helper it_damage_inline, then used PAD_STACK(0x8) wrappers in the exported callbacks to preserve matching-sensitive stack behavior.
Used explicit state transitions through Item_80268E5C and the item state table to encode motion-state behavior rather than leaving callback relationships implicit.
Preserved matching-sensitive details such as PAD_STACK(0x50) in the spawn path and PAD_STACK(0x8) around damage-style callbacks.
Handled facing direction consistently by setting both Item.facing_dir and mpCollSetFacingDir based on the float direction.
decomp_lessons: When matching item files, update configure.py from NonMatching to Matching only alongside complete callback implementations and headers.
Item-specific xDD4 data should be represented by a typed *_ItemVars struct and added to the Item union once offsets are understood.
Article special attributes should get a named attribute struct in itCommonItems.h when multiple fields are read in behavior code.
Matching item callbacks may require exact return types in headers, even for functions whose bodies do not visibly return a value in the excerpt.
Small wrapper functions with PAD_STACK can be necessary when several externally referenced callbacks share one inline body.
State tables are central evidence for item motion callback ordering; matching work should keep table entries and callback prototypes synchronized.
Spawning item articles often requires initializing facing, collision facing direction, item flags, per-item vars, velocity, and initial motion state in a tightly ordered sequence.
naming_conventions: Struct names follow the item-specific pattern itKyasarinEgg_ItemVars and itKyasarinEggAttributes.
Per-item union member uses lower camel case kyasarinEgg.
Logic callbacks use the pattern itKyasarinEgg_Logic28_PickedUp, Dropped, Thrown, and ShieldBounced.
Unidentified motion callbacks retain address/motion-style names such as itKyasarinegg_UnkMotion0_Coll and itKyasarinegg_UnkMotion3_Phys.
Unidentified exported functions retain address names such as it_802EFA44, it_802EFB0C, it_802EFCC0, it_802EFD84, and it_802F022C.
The PR preserves the existing mixed spelling/casing distinction between file/function prefixes itkyasarinegg, itKyasarinegg, and itKyasarinEgg rather than normalizing it without evidence.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Changed the object to Matching in configure.py after matching the source.
Used PAD_STACK(0x50) in it_802EFA44 and PAD_STACK(0x8) in several damage-interaction wrappers to satisfy stack layout.
Factored repeated damage behavior into inline bool it_damage_inline(Item_GObj* gobj), likely to reproduce compiler inlining across multiple callbacks.
Kept magic constants from the matched binary where names were not established, including item kind 0xEC, effect id 0x4d0, sound/effect args 0xf4/0x7f/0x40, and state IDs 0 through 4.
Used HSD_Randi(ap->x10) to reproduce randomized outcome behavior in the damage path.
Matched callback signatures in the header to the implementation, including int collision callbacks and bool animation/damage callbacks.
review_feedback: No review comments were included in the provided PR context.

The diff contains an inline author/code comment near Item_8026AE84: "why is this being inlined?", suggesting uncertainty about the matching behavior of that call, but no reviewer discussion is available in the slice.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename it_802EFA44, it_802EFB0C, it_802EFCC0, it_802EFD84, or it_802F022C.
Search for follow-up discussion about the inline comment "why is this being inlined?" near Item_8026AE84.
Compare Kyasarin egg item state table patterns with other common item article eggs or projectiles.
Search for subsequent refinements to itKyasarinEggAttributes field names x4, x8, xC, and x10.
Check whether itKyasarinegg_UnkMotion1_Coll returning int without an explicit return was later cleaned up or required for matching.
